methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
WeaveGrid is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Apoorv Bhargava and John Taggart that has raised $78M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $78M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $148M. Peer-median analysis references a cohort of 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M (range: $120M–$5.0B). The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier energy.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $126M to $408M, with a median of $252M. This range reflects material uncertainty around WeaveGrid's relative positioning within the peer set. Key variables affecting valuation within this band include revenue traction, technical risk mitigation since the $78M raise, and capital efficiency relative to peers. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound conservatively; peer comparables drive the upper bound but exhibit significant dispersion, warranting skepticism of outlier-driven valuations at either extreme.
